data.title: iBio (NASDAQ:IBIO) reports FY2025 financial results, ending June 30, 2025, and corporate progress update.
data.description: On September 5, 2025, iBio, Inc., a company listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol IBIO, shared its financial results for the fiscal year ending on June 30, 2025. The company, known for its AI-driven precision antibody therapies, revealed its performance for the year and gave insights into its corporate advancements in San Diego.

data.title: US to launch public consultations on renegotiating the USMCA trade pact, with process to begin in next month. President Trump's team seeks input from companies and unions, with a mandatory six-year review. Trilateral USMCA review set for July 1, 2026.
data.description: The Trump administration is preparing to renegotiate the US-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), the largest free trade deal for the U.S. The process is anticipated to start in the next month. The Office of the U.S. Trade Representative will initiate public consultations for the renegotiation as required by a 2020 law implementing the pact. These consultations must begin by Oct. 4 and are expected to be the initial phase of a lengthy renegotiation process. President Trump's team will gather feedback from companies and unions this week. The renegotiation is projected to include a mandatory six-year review, with the first official trilateral USMCA review scheduled for July 1, 2026. Trump signed the USMCA in 2020, replacing the 1992... (More details available on Benzinga)

data.title: Bitcoin hits $112,000; Ethereum, Dogecoin, XRP consolidate. 95,307 traders liquidated, losing $267.77M in 24 hrs. $227.5M outflows from Bitcoin ETFs.
data.description: On Friday morning, the cryptocurrency market saw a slight increase with Bitcoin climbing back over $112,000, currently priced at $112,356.29. Ethereum is at $4,420.26, Solana at $206.15, XRP at $2.84, Dogecoin at $0.2166, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001230. Data from Coinglass revealed that 95,307 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses totaling $267.77 million over the past day. SoSoValue data indicated that there were net outflows of $227.5 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs on Wednesday, while Ethereum ETFs saw net outflows of $167.4 million. Crypto trader Jelle observed that Bitcoin's climb above $112,000 is significant after two weeks below this level, as it now aims to establish this as a support level. More information can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock market slightly up with Nvidia falling on earnings, Snowflake, CrowdStrike, Urban Outfitters active. Dow Jones Futures Rise.
data.description: Stocks in the market saw a slight increase in anticipation of Nvidia's earnings report. Snowflake, CrowdStrike, and Urban Outfitters also experienced heightened activity. Dow Jones Futures rose post-market, with Nvidia showing a decline following its earnings release, making it a significant mover in the market.

data.title: H&R Block CEO Jeffrey J. Jones II retiring on Dec. 31, 2025; Curtis Campbell to succeed him in 2026.
data.description: Kansas City, Missouri - On August 11, 2025, H&R Block, Inc. (NYSE: HRB) disclosed that Jeffrey J. Jones II will retire as President and CEO on December 31, 2025. Jones will stay on the Board until then and will work as a Strategic Advisor until September 2026. Curtis Campbell, the current President of Global Consumer Tax and Chief Product Officer, will take over as President and CEO from January 1, 2026 onwards.
